title: Lara Nathans
content:
Partner | Co-Leader of the National Retail and Consumer Markets Group
People.ContactEmail lnathans@mccarthy.ca
With extensive retail and consumer markets industry expertise, Lara leverages her industry experience to help clients cut through complexities to get deals done
Lara Nathans is a partner in the firm’s Business Law Group and Founder and Co-Leader of our National Retail and Consumer Markets Group. Lara is a trusted advisor to consumer-facing companies looking to establish or grow in the Canadian market. She advises global companies entering the Canadian market (whether through M&A, e-commerce, bricks and mortar or a combination) on their market entry strategies and related legal issues.
A highly connected and influential lawyer in the field, Lara brings market intelligence, her global network of trusted relationships and knowledge of the industry to bear for clients, introducing them to not only what, but whom they need to know to achieve their goals. She excels at negotiating mergers and acquisitions as well as corporate reorganizations and commercial matters. Practical in her approach, Lara provides well-informed guidance on entry strategies and legal issues, including securities law requirements, such as continuous disclosure and governance matters.
In addition to her M&A and commercial experience, Lara advises merchants and other consumer-facing businesses on their credit card and loyalty programs, including complex co-branding and linked loyalty arrangements.

#### Establishing and growing your place in the competitive Canadian market
Lara’s vision for clients’ businesses is based on a thorough understanding of their industries, challenges and opportunities. It is the hallmark of her practice, with the end-goal of helping them achieve success and remain competitive.
Lara’s work in establishing the Retail and Consumer Markets Group allows the firm to seamlessly support clients with both game-changing and day-to-day issues. She also developed McCarthy Tétrault’s pioneering industry strategy, aligning our firm across practices and regions to serve our clients in Canada’s key sectors that are shaping the global economy. This experience gives Lara a unique understanding of our clients’ needs and their industries, which she has translated into effective guidance for our clients and leadership of our Retail and Consumer Markets Group.
Powered by market intelligence, a global network of trusted relationships and industry focus, Lara is relentless in her pursuit of client success at McCarthy Tétrault.

#### Completing Retail and Consumer Markets Transactions
Regarded as a skillful negotiator and steadfast client advocate, Lara has played an integral role in numerous high-profile transactions, including:
- Corby Spirit and Wine Limited on its acquisition of Ace Beverage Group Inc.;
- Smucker Foods of Canada Corp.’s sale of its Bick's® pickles, Habitant® pickled beets, Woodman's® horseradish and McLarens® pickled onions brands to TreeHouse Foods Canada, Ltd. for US$20 million
- Several cross-border retailers on their credit card and loyalty programs
- LL Bean on its entry into Canada and agreement with The Jaytex Group;
- A major department store on all aspects of its entry into Canada and its Canadian e-commerce launch;
- Home Depot’s US$265 million acquisition of Compact Power and its acquisition of Askuity;
- A global jewellery retailer on its expansion into Canada by acquisition of a national jewellery retailer;
- Fyffes’ C$145 million acquisition of Highline Produce Limited;
- Nestle’s US$5.75 billion acquisition of the core brands of The Bountiful Company, US$7.15 billion strategic alliance with Starbucks and its US$2.3 billion acquisition of Atrium Innovations Inc.;
- Corby Spirit and Wine’s acquisitions of Domaines Pinnacle Inc. and Foreign Affair Winery and its agreements with Pernod Ricard S.A..

#### At the forefront of retail trends and a thought leader in the community
With her deep industry knowledge, Lara is a sought-after expert in the retail, food and beverage and consumer products transactions field, speaking regularly on business and legal issues affecting consumer-facing businesses. Her appearances include the World Retail Congress, the Retail Industry Leaders Association’s Retail Law Conference, the World Department Store Forum, the Luxury Law Summit, Food and Consumer Products of Canada and our firm’s Annual Retail and Consumer Markets Summit.
With a long-time commitment to community service, Lara serves on the United Way of Greater Toronto’s Major Giving Cabinet, the Supplier Advisory Committee of The Home Depot of Canada Foundation and the Board of Governors of The Sterling Hall School, and has served on the board of directors of local charitable groups Sheena’s Place and the Anne Johnston Health Station.
She received her LLB from Queen’s University in 2001.
